label: Subject, Product, or Update
placeholder: What is the post about?
helper: Enter the main topic, product, event, lesson, announcement, or professional update.
why: It establishes the central subject of the post.
display: Always visible

label: Primary Goal
placeholder: Select the main goal
helper: Choose the single most important result the post should support.
why: The primary goal determines the post’s structure, emphasis, and CTA.
display: Always visible

label: Target Audience
placeholder: Example: freelance designers, SaaS founders, HR managers
helper: Describe the professionals or decision-makers the post should speak to.
why: Audience information materially changes vocabulary, examples, depth, and positioning.
display: Always visible

label: Core Message
placeholder: What should readers understand, remember, or believe after reading?
helper: State the central insight, lesson, argument, announcement, or value proposition.
why: It prevents the model from guessing the intended takeaway.
display: Always visible

label: Facts and Key Details
placeholder: Add names, dates, features, results, examples, numbers, or confirmed details.
helper: Include only facts that may safely appear in the post; write “No external claims” when appropriate.
why: It provides the factual basis and reduces unsupported claims or invented details.
display: Always visible

label: Post Format
placeholder: Choose a format
helper: Select a structure or let the model choose the best fit for the goal.
why: Different LinkedIn post types require meaningfully different narrative structures.
display: Always visible

label: Reader Action
placeholder: Choose the preferred action
helper: Select one primary next step or let the model decide whether a CTA is useful.
why: It controls the post’s conclusion without requiring the user to write a CTA.
display: Always visible

label: Output Language
placeholder: Example: English, Ukrainian, German
helper: Enter the language in which the complete post should be written.
why: Language cannot always be safely inferred from the topic or interface.
display: Always visible

label: Post Length
placeholder: Choose a preferred length
helper: The model will prioritize completeness and remain below the platform limit.
why: Length materially affects depth, pacing, and mobile readability.
display: Always visible

label: Publishing Perspective
placeholder: Choose who is publishing
helper: Controls whether the post sounds personal, employee-led, or organizational.
why: Personal profiles and company Pages require different pronouns, credibility cues, and levels of formality.
display: Always visible

label: Audience Context
placeholder: What does this audience already know about the subject?
helper: Add relevant experience level, industry context, or current situation.
why: It helps prevent content from being too basic, technical, or disconnected from the reader’s reality.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group

label: Audience Problem or Interest
placeholder: What problem, question, or opportunity matters most to them?
helper: Explain why the audience should care about this post now.
why: It connects the subject to a concrete reader need rather than producing a self-focused post.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group

label: Credibility Context
placeholder: Add relevant experience, role, process, or verified result.
helper: Provide only credibility details that may truthfully appear in the post.
why: It allows credible authority signals without encouraging invented expertise.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group

label: Reader Questions or Objections
placeholder: What might readers doubt, misunderstand, or disagree with?
helper: The model may address relevant concerns naturally within the post.
why: It improves persuasion and clarity when the topic may generate predictable resistance.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group

label: Tone
placeholder: Choose a tone
helper: Select the general communication style without controlling every sentence.
why: Tone meaningfully changes how the same message is perceived.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group

label: Brand Voice Notes
placeholder: Example: practical, slightly informal, no motivational language
helper: Add distinctive voice preferences that cannot be inferred from the topic.
why: It supports recognizable brand style without creating several overlapping voice fields.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group

label: Emoji Use
placeholder: Choose emoji use
helper: Controls whether emojis are omitted or used sparingly.
why: Emoji preferences vary significantly between professional brands and personal creators.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group

label: Hashtag Use
placeholder: Choose hashtag behavior
helper: Auto adds hashtags only when they provide clear relevance or discovery value.
why: Hashtags may be useful for some professional topics but inappropriate or distracting for others.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group

label: Source Material
placeholder: Paste approved notes, research, product information, or source excerpts.
helper: The model may summarize this material but must not invent missing information.
why: It provides additional evidence and detailed context for fact-sensitive posts.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group

label: Destination Link
placeholder: https://example.com/page
helper: Add the exact page readers should visit when the post requires a link.
why: The model must not invent URLs or guess the intended destination.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group

label: Affiliation or Disclosure
placeholder: Example: I built this product; sponsored by; affiliate relationship
helper: Add any ownership, employment, sponsorship, or commercial relationship that should be disclosed.
why: It prevents promotional content from being presented as an independent recommendation.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group

label: Privacy Restrictions
placeholder: List names, client details, metrics, or internal information that must not appear.
helper: The model will omit or generalize protected and confidential details.
why: Professional posts may involve clients, employees, internal data, or personal information.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group

label: Claims or Wording to Avoid
placeholder: Example: no guarantees, no competitor comparisons, avoid “best”
helper: List prohibited claims, sensitive terminology, or legal and brand restrictions.
why: It protects against known brand, regulatory, contractual, or factual risks.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group

label: Additional Requirements
placeholder: Add any remaining context or formatting requirement.
helper: Use this only for relevant instructions not covered by the other fields.
why: It provides one controlled place for legitimate requirements without creating miscellaneous duplicate fields.
display: Always visible within the collapsed group
